task_text: Prepare a comprehensive project health briefing for NovaBridge's leadership team. The briefing should cover: current progress, key risks, upcoming Q3 commitments, and any issues that need leadership attention. Save it as project_health_briefing.txt.

Project Helios — Weekly Status Update
Week of 2026-07-06

Overall: ON TRACK (minor risks)

Milestone Progress:
- Data ingestion module: 85% complete
- API gateway: 100% complete (delivered)
- Client dashboard: 60% complete
- Integration testing: 40% complete

Sprint 14 Goals:
- Complete dashboard module v1
- Resolve data format inconsistencies (Blocker #1)
- Begin UAT preparation

Open Blockers:
1. Meridian source data contains inconsistent date formats (ISO 8601 vs. MM/DD/YYYY).
   Dev team awaiting clarification from Meridian IT. Estimated delay: 2-3 days.
2. Third-party mapping library (GeoSync v2.1) license renewal pending. Legal reviewing.
   Target resolution: 2026-07-14.

Next milestone: Integration Test Sign-off — target 2026-07-25

Project Helios — Q2 Deliverables Checklist

[DONE]      Requirements documentation v1.0
[DONE]      System architecture design
[DONE]      API gateway implementation
[DONE]      Development environment setup
[DONE]      Data schema finalization
[IN-PROGRESS] Data ingestion module (target: 2026-07-18)
[IN-PROGRESS] Client dashboard v1 (target: 2026-07-22)
[OVERDUE]   Integration test suite setup (was due 2026-06-30; rescheduled to 2026-07-20)
[OVERDUE]   UAT environment provisioning (was due 2026-07-01; blocked by Meridian IT access)
[OVERDUE]   Performance benchmarking report (was due 2026-07-05; owner: Derek Okafor)

Project Helios — Q3 Roadmap
INTERNAL DRAFT — Not for client distribution
Last updated: 2026-07-06

Q3 2026 Priorities:

1. COMPLETE SYSTEM INTEGRATION (July)
   - Finalize data ingestion module (target: 2026-07-18)
   - Complete client dashboard v1 (target: 2026-07-22)
   - Integration test sign-off (target: 2026-07-25)

2. PRODUCT DEMO FOR MERIDIAN (July 25)
   - Live data ingestion demonstration using Meridian sample data
   - End-to-end pipeline walkthrough
   - Dashboard visualization with real data (per contract Section 9.3)

3. USER ACCEPTANCE TESTING — MERIDIAN GROUP (August 17-28)
   - Deploy to UAT environment
   - Support Meridian user testing sessions
   - Address UAT feedback (budget: 2 sprints)

4. PERFORMANCE HARDENING (August-September)
   - Dashboard load time target: <2 seconds for 100k records
   - Ingestion throughput benchmarking (target: 500k records/min sustained)
   - External security penetration test (vendor TBD)

5. LAUNCH PREPARATION (September)
   - Production environment setup and runbook documentation
   - Hypercare plan finalization (30-day post-launch model)
   - Final client training sessions

Target launch date: 2026-10-01
Key risk: UAT slippage of >2 weeks pushes launch into Q4, triggering Section 7.1 penalty review.

Proposed Q3 feature additions (subject to change control approval):
- Scheduled ingestion jobs (cron-based triggers)
- Email alerting for pipeline failures
- Self-service mapping rule editor (deferred from Q2)

Project Helios — Risk Register
Last reviewed: 2026-07-07 | Owner: Sarah Chen (PM)

ID    Risk Description                             Likelihood  Impact  Score  Mitigation Owner    Status
----  -------------------------------------------  ----------  ------  -----  ------------------  --------
R-01  Meridian source data quality issues           High        High    9      Amir Hassan         Active — date format blocker confirmed in sprint 14
R-02  GeoSync license not renewed before demo       Medium      High    6      Raj Patel           Active — legal review in progress, due 2026-07-14
R-03  Dashboard performance on large datasets       Medium      High    6      Derek Okafor        Active — benchmark task HEL-247 not yet started
R-04  UAT timeline slippage                        High        Medium  6      Sarah Chen          Active — 1-week extension already consumed
R-05  Key person dependency: Derek Okafor           Low         High    3      Sarah Chen          Monitored — no backup tech lead identified
R-06  Meridian IT environment access delays        Medium      Medium  4      Nadia Petrov        Active — awaiting IT infrastructure diagram
R-07  Scope creep from Meridian feature requests   Low         Medium  2      Raj Patel           Monitored — 3 informal requests logged, not yet escalated
R-08  Cloud cost overrun                           Low         Low     1      Nadia Petrov        Monitored
R-09  Third-party API deprecation (non-GeoSync)    Very Low    Medium  1      Olivia Brooks       Monitored

Meeting Notes: Project Helios / Meridian Group Sync
Date: 2026-07-08
Attendees: Sarah Chen (NovaBridge PM), Derek Okafor (NovaBridge Tech Lead),
           Priya Kapoor (Meridian IT Lead), James Whitfield (Meridian Project Sponsor)

Decisions:
1. Demo date confirmed: 2026-07-25 (product demo for Meridian stakeholders)
2. Meridian will provide clean sample dataset by 2026-07-15 to unblock date format issue
3. UAT period extended by 1 week (now Aug 17-28)

Action Items:
- NovaBridge: Resolve GeoSync license before demo (owner: Derek Okafor, due 2026-07-14)
- NovaBridge: Send draft demo agenda to Meridian by 2026-07-18 (owner: Sarah Chen)
- Meridian: Share IT infrastructure diagram for integration environment setup
  (owner: Priya Kapoor, due 2026-07-12)

Notes:
- James Whitfield emphasized that the demo must include live data ingestion, not mock data.
- Concern raised about dashboard load times on large datasets. Derek to benchmark before demo.
- Priya Kapoor confirmed Meridian IT will provision UAT environment access by 2026-07-19.

Project Helios — QA Testing Log: Sprint 14
QA Engineer: Kenji Watanabe

=== Test Suite: Authentication Flows ===

Suite result: 1/4 complete

=== Open Defects ===
HEL-D-014  Date normalization fails for MM/DD/YYYY input format.
           Affects ~40% of Meridian source data. Severity: HIGH.
           Owner: Amir Hassan. Blocked pending Meridian sample data (due 2026-07-15).

HEL-D-015  Large file ingestion (>200k records) causes memory overflow.
           Pipeline crashes at approximately 210k record mark. Severity: HIGH.
           Owner: Amir Hassan. Root cause under investigation.

Overall sprint 14 test status: 10/15 test cases executed, 2 FAIL (open), 3 not yet run.

Project Helios — Q2 Budget vs. Actuals
Prepared by: NovaBridge Finance | Date: 2026-07-07

Category,Q2 Budget ($),Q2 Actual ($),Variance ($),Notes
Engineering (internal),180000,194200,+14200,Overrun: unplanned sprint added in May
Subcontractors,45000,51800,+6800,GeoSync integration scoping work
Cloud infrastructure,28000,31500,+3500,Load testing environment spun up early
QA and testing,22000,20100,-1900,Under budget; QA resource joined 2 weeks late
Travel and client meetings,12000,9400,-2600,Two site visits deferred to Q3
Contingency reserve,15000,15000,0,Untouched
TOTAL,302000,322000,+20000,

Projected additional Q3 overruns:
- GeoSync license (if not renewed before expiry): $12,000
- UAT extension (1 additional week): $15,000
- Estimated total H2 projected overrun: $47,000

Note: Per MSA Section 4.2, internal cost overruns cannot be passed to Meridian Group.
CFO review scheduled: 2026-07-18
Action required: Budget reconciliation plan due to CFO by 2026-07-20.

input.content:
NovaBridge Project Health Briefing — Project Helios
Prepared for: NovaBridge Leadership Team
Date: 2026-07-11

EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
Project Helios remains officially ON TRACK, but with meaningful schedule, quality, licensing, and budget risks emerging as the team enters Q3 execution. Core platform work is progressing: the API gateway is complete, data ingestion is 85% complete, dashboard v1 is 60% complete, and integration testing is 40% complete. However, several critical dependencies must be resolved in the next two weeks to protect the July 25 Meridian product demo, the August UAT window, and the October 1 target launch.

Leadership attention is recommended on four areas: Meridian data quality and access dependencies, GeoSync licensing, dashboard/ingestion performance, and Q2/Q3 budget pressure. UAT has already been extended by one week, and further slippage of more than two weeks could push launch into Q4 and trigger a Section 7.1 penalty review.


1. CURRENT PROGRESS

Overall status: ON TRACK with minor-to-moderate risks increasing.

Milestone and workstream progress:
- Requirements documentation v1.0: Complete.
- System architecture design: Complete.
- Development environment setup: Complete.
- Data schema finalization: Complete.
- API gateway implementation: Complete and delivered.
- Data ingestion module: 85% complete; target completion 2026-07-18.
- Client dashboard v1: 60% complete; target completion 2026-07-22.
- Integration testing: 40% complete; integration test sign-off target 2026-07-25.

Q2 deliverables status:
- Completed: requirements, architecture, API gateway, dev environment, and data schema.
- In progress: data ingestion module and client dashboard v1.
- Overdue: integration test suite setup, UAT environment provisioning, and performance benchmarking report.

Sprint 14 / QA status:
- Authentication flows: 4/4 test cases passing.
- Data pipeline batch ingestion: 5/7 passing, 2 failing.
- Dashboard testing: 1/4 complete; key chart and load-time tests remain incomplete.
- Overall Sprint 14 test execution: 10/15 test cases executed, with 2 open high-severity failures and 3 not yet run.

Open high-severity defects:
- HEL-D-014: Date normalization fails for MM/DD/YYYY input. This affects approximately 40% of Meridian source data and is blocked pending Meridian sample data due 2026-07-15.
- HEL-D-015: Large file ingestion above approximately 200k records causes memory overflow. Root cause is under investigation.

Client alignment:
- Meridian product demo confirmed for 2026-07-25.
- Meridian sponsor explicitly requires live data ingestion, not mock data, for the demo.
- Meridian committed to provide clean sample dataset by 2026-07-15.
- Meridian IT is expected to provision UAT environment access by 2026-07-19 and provide infrastructure diagram by 2026-07-12.


2. KEY RISKS

Highest-priority active risks:

1. Meridian source data quality issues
- Likelihood: High; Impact: High; Risk score: 9.
- Current issue: inconsistent date formats across Meridian source data, including ISO 8601 and MM/DD/YYYY.
- Business impact: blocks reliable data ingestion and threatens the July 25 live demo.
- Current mitigation: Meridian to provide clean sample dataset by 2026-07-15; Amir Hassan owns technical remediation.

2. GeoSync license renewal pending
- Likelihood: Medium; Impact: High; Risk score: 6.
- Current issue: third-party mapping library GeoSync v2.1 license renewal is under legal review.
- Due date: 2026-07-14.
- Business impact: could affect demo readiness and mapping functionality.
- Current mitigation: legal review in progress; NovaBridge action item to resolve before demo.

3. Dashboard and ingestion performance risk
- Likelihood: Medium; Impact: High; Risk score: 6.
- Current issue: dashboard performance for large datasets remains unproven; benchmarking task HEL-247 has not yet started. Large file ingestion currently fails around 210k records due to memory overflow.
- Q3 targets: dashboard load time under 2 seconds for 100k records; sustained ingestion throughput of 500k records/min.
- Business impact: demo credibility, UAT success, and launch readiness are at risk.

4. UAT timeline slippage
- Likelihood: High; Impact: Medium; Risk score: 6.
- Current issue: UAT has already been extended by one week and is now scheduled for 2026-08-17 through 2026-08-28.
- Business impact: slippage of more than two weeks could push launch into Q4 and trigger Section 7.1 penalty review.

5. Meridian IT environment access delays
- Likelihood: Medium; Impact: Medium; Risk score: 4.
- Current issue: UAT environment provisioning and integration setup depend on Meridian IT access and infrastructure details.
- Key dates: infrastructure diagram due 2026-07-12; UAT access expected by 2026-07-19.

6. Key person dependency
- Likelihood: Low; Impact: High; Risk score: 3.
- Current issue: no backup technical lead identified for Derek Okafor.
- Business impact: continuity risk if performance benchmarking, demo preparation, or technical escalation relies too heavily on a single individual.

7. Scope creep
- Likelihood: Low; Impact: Medium; Risk score: 2.
- Current issue: three informal Meridian feature requests logged but not yet escalated.
- Proposed Q3 additions require change control approval: scheduled ingestion jobs, email alerting for pipeline failures, and self-service mapping rule editor.

Budget risk:
- Q2 budget: $302,000.
- Q2 actual: $322,000.
- Q2 overrun: $20,000.
- Primary drivers: unplanned sprint work, GeoSync integration scoping, and early load-testing environment usage.
- Projected additional Q3 overruns: $12,000 for GeoSync license if not renewed before expiry; $15,000 for the one-week UAT extension.
- Estimated total H2 projected overrun: $47,000.
- Per MSA Section 4.2, internal cost overruns cannot be passed to Meridian.


3. UPCOMING Q3 COMMITMENTS

July commitments:
- 2026-07-12: Meridian to share IT infrastructure diagram for integration environment setup.
- 2026-07-14: Resolve GeoSync license renewal.
- 2026-07-15: Meridian to provide clean sample dataset to unblock date-format issue.
- 2026-07-18: Finalize data ingestion module.
- 2026-07-18: Send draft demo agenda to Meridian.
- 2026-07-19: Meridian IT to provision UAT environment access.
- 2026-07-20: Integration test suite setup rescheduled completion.
- 2026-07-20: Budget reconciliation plan due to CFO.
- 2026-07-22: Complete client dashboard v1.
- 2026-07-25: Integration test sign-off target.
- 2026-07-25: Product demo for Meridian, including live data ingestion, end-to-end pipeline walkthrough, and dashboard visualization with real data.

August commitments:
- 2026-08-17 to 2026-08-28: Meridian UAT period.
- Support Meridian user testing sessions.
- Address UAT feedback, with two sprints budgeted for remediation.
- Continue performance hardening for dashboard load time and ingestion throughput.

August-September commitments:
- Dashboard load time target: under 2 seconds for 100k records.
- Ingestion throughput benchmarking target: 500k records/min sustained.
- External security penetration test; vendor still TBD.

September commitments:
- Production environment setup.
- Runbook documentation.
- Hypercare plan finalization for 30-day post-launch support model.
- Final client training sessions.

Launch commitment:
- Target launch date: 2026-10-01.


4. ISSUES REQUIRING LEADERSHIP ATTENTION

1. Escalate Meridian dependency management
Leadership should reinforce with Meridian executive stakeholders that clean sample data, infrastructure details, and UAT access are critical-path dependencies. The current dates are tight: sample data is due 2026-07-15, infrastructure diagram due 2026-07-12, and UAT access expected 2026-07-19. Missed dates will directly threaten the July 25 demo and UAT readiness.

2. Resolve GeoSync licensing by 2026-07-14
The GeoSync license is a high-impact risk. Leadership may need to support Legal and Procurement escalation to ensure renewal before the demo window. If renewal cannot be completed, the team needs an approved contingency plan immediately.

3. Direct performance recovery plan
Large file ingestion currently fails near 210k records, and dashboard load testing for 100k records has not yet been completed. These gaps are significant because the demo requires live data ingestion and Q3 performance targets are aggressive. Leadership should request a short written recovery plan covering defect HEL-D-015, benchmark task HEL-247, dashboard load testing, owners, and daily milestones through July 25.

4. Protect UAT and launch schedule
UAT has already consumed a one-week extension. Any additional slippage of more than two weeks could move launch into Q4 and trigger penalty review under Section 7.1. Leadership should require weekly UAT readiness checkpoints through August and a formal go/no-go assessment before UAT starts.

5. Address budget overrun and reconciliation
The project is $20,000 over Q2 budget, with estimated H2 overrun exposure of $47,000. Since internal overruns cannot be passed to Meridian, leadership should review the budget reconciliation plan before the CFO deadline of 2026-07-20 and decide whether to approve contingency usage, reduce scope, or absorb the overrun.

6. Assign backup technical leadership
Derek Okafor is a key dependency for performance benchmarking, demo readiness, and technical decisions. Leadership should assign a backup technical lead or deputy immediately to reduce delivery continuity risk.

7. Enforce change control on Q3 feature additions
Proposed additions — scheduled ingestion jobs, email alerting for pipeline failures, and self-service mapping rule editor — should not enter Q3 delivery without formal change control. Leadership should align Sales, Product, and Delivery on whether any of these are committed, deferred, or separately priced.


5. RECOMMENDED NEXT ACTIONS

Immediate actions for the next 72 hours:
- Confirm Meridian infrastructure diagram receipt by 2026-07-12; escalate if missed.
- Obtain a definitive GeoSync renewal decision by 2026-07-14.
- Confirm Meridian sample dataset delivery path and validation criteria before 2026-07-15.
- Start or accelerate performance benchmarking for large file ingestion and dashboard load time.
- Identify backup technical lead for Derek Okafor.

Actions before July 25 demo:
- Close HEL-D-014 or document accepted workaround for MM/DD/YYYY data.
- Resolve HEL-D-015 or reduce demo data volume only with leadership/client approval.
- Complete dashboard v1 and remaining dashboard QA tests.
- Complete integration test suite setup and target integration sign-off.
- Send demo agenda to Meridian by 2026-07-18.
- Prepare a client-safe demo risk script and fallback plan.

Actions before UAT:
- Verify UAT environment access and connectivity.
- Confirm UAT support staffing and defect triage model.
- Establish go/no-go criteria for UAT start.
- Lock change control scope for UAT period.


6. OVERALL HEALTH ASSESSMENT

Current rating: Yellow / Watch Closely.

Rationale: Core delivery remains viable and several major components are complete, but the project is entering a compressed period with critical client dependencies, high-severity ingestion defects, unproven performance, licensing uncertainty, and budget pressure. If the team resolves the sample data issue, GeoSync license, and ingestion performance defects by mid-to-late July, the July demo and August UAT remain achievable. If these issues slip, launch risk will increase materially and leadership intervention may be required to reset scope, schedule, or commercial expectations.

content.text: Done. I prepared the comprehensive project health briefing and saved it as `project_health_briefing.txt`.
